question: Visual Line of Sight (VLOS) is defined as?

options.text: The remote pilot in command, and visual observers can see the aircraft with vision aided by binoculars.
Only visual observers are required to see the aircraft with vision unaided by any device other than corrective lenses (spectacles or contact lenses).
The remote pilot in command, and visual observers can see the aircraft for at least 50% of the time.
The remote pilot in command, and visual observers can see the aircraft with vision unaided by any device other than corrective lenses (spectacles or contact lenses).

classification_notes: VLOS definition is an ANR-101 operational requirement.
